title: Strict TSConfig, TypeScript 7 Defaults, and Project Graphs · Type Recall
summary: Create a clean multi-package configuration that fails on unsafe boundaries, unused work, and incompatible module assumptions. Learners retrieve rules and procedures before seeing feedback. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for a strict TypeScript 7 project configuration after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Use strict, noUncheckedIndexedAccess, exactOptionalPropertyTypes, useUnknownInCatchVariables, and override checks from actual risk.
Account for TypeScript 7 strict, target, module, rootDir, types, and side-effect import defaults without legacy no-op flags.
Predict root files from files, include, exclude, imports, types, and library selection.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Retrieve the complete evidence cycle

Competency probe: Use strict, noUncheckedIndexedAccess, exactOptionalPropertyTypes, useUnknownInCatchVariables, and override checks from actual risk.
